register.note: No specimen hero present: hero.png opens on a title+lede with a metadata placard and a large empty void right of the lede, where ciechanowski-watch/00 lands a composed, poster-worthy 3D watch directly under its lede.

No hero exists to depict the mechanism, so unlike seeing-theory-regression/01 where the residual squares and fitted line ARE the opening picture, the masthead shows no XOR boundary at all until you scroll into the spine.

The See-it canvas does carry its own labels (title strip 'ONE NEURON — A STRAIGHT LINE XOR DEFEATS', live '75% ON XOR', axes x1/x2) and colour-tints key prose terms, but it offers one headline+one stat where distill-momentum/02 fuses five named, separately-captioned small-multiples and inline math into the figure itself.

Calm, warm, AA-clean and Distill-soft with no aliasing, but the masthead carries a conspicuous dead-air void right of the lede and the XOR scatter is a flat banded blue/tan split with plain dots — competent, not the dimensional, finished render ciechanowski-watch/02 sustains.

Run-it has a learner-controlled Train/Reset transport whose boundary-morph is genuinely explanatory (not decorative), but from statics I cannot verify stepping/scrubbing of the training, so it does not reach the choreographed, steppable descent 3b1b-gradient-descent/02 implies.

Semantic grammar holds cleanly — Break-it keeps the blue prediction-field / amber truth-points and tints 'memorise the noise' error-red only as a weighted+underlined prose term rather than draining the canvas red, matching r2d3-trees/00's restrained class-colour discipline.

hero.note: A hero would need a full-content-width XOR specimen showing the bent decision boundary (the mechanism) with a mono figcaption, replacing the empty void beside the lede so the page has a poster-worthy, thumbnail-legible face.

assessment.note: Item 04 is a real hands-on LAB TASK, but the item explicitly labelled '05 TRANSFER' resolves the novel double-the-size scenario via a 3-option MCQ stack rather than an interaction or open prompt.

blocking: No specimen hero: add a full-width XOR boundary portrait under the lede (mechanism-in-the-picture) to fill the masthead void and give the page a thumbnail-legible face.
Lift annotation density on the See-it canvas toward distill-momentum/02 — in-figure callouts/leader-lines on the boundary and per-quadrant labels, not just one title strip plus a single stat.
Make the '05 TRANSFER' item interactive or open (e.g. let the learner act on the failing network) instead of a 3-option MCQ, since transfer is the loop's payoff.
verdict.summary: Disciplined colour and a real learning loop, but no hero and only competent annotation/atmosphere/motion keep it below the benchmark — does not clear register 3.
